what_it_does: This file is a Round 2 verification finding that checks whether the design-lint FORM gate fires on the guided-review-surface frontend when it is built through the operator-approve → dispatch_decision → autonomous-Claude → commit path. It answers yes for wire-routed builds, identifies a gap for edits that bypass dispatch_decision, reports existing off-token debt, and proposes a tracked pre-commit hook fix.
observations: Every claim is tagged as Observed, Verified, or Inferred under an Evidence Classification Protocol.
The FORM gate is design/_system/check.py run with --target and --fail-on; in design-lint mode it scans .tsx/.css for hardcoded off-token colour literals and bespoke elements.
The bespoke-elements check is currently a stub that returns [], so the gate only catches colour literals today.
The guided-review-surface build path is described as operator-approve → dispatch_decision → implement.launch → verify → commit.
_design_critic is called unconditionally inside dispatch_decision after launch and after the injectable verifier, making the FORM gate non-bypassable for wire-routed builds.
Only files under canvas/ with .tsx or .css extensions are linted, matching _SURFACE_PREFIXES = ("canvas/",).
Changed files are captured from git diff ground truth, not from the model's self-report.
There is no active git hook, no CI pipeline, and no npm lint step, so FE written outside dispatch_decision is never FORM-checked.
A live run of check.py on canvas/app/src found two off-token #fff literals at app.css:1426 and app.css:1515.
The acceptance test is hermetic and uses planted temp files, so it does not lint the live canvas/app/src source.
The proposed fix is a tracked hooks/pre-commit bash hook that lints only staged canvas .tsx/.css files, wired via git config core.hooksPath hooks and documented in AGENTS.md.
The file explicitly marks the proposed hook as code-complete but untested.
standouts: The FORM gate is structurally sound inside dispatch_decision but completely absent at the universal commit boundary.
Live canvas/app/src already carries two #fff off-token literals that the current controls cannot catch retroactively.
The proposed pre-commit hook deliberately lints only staged files to avoid blocking clean commits with pre-existing debt.
conventions: Uses file:line citations for Observed claims and execution results for Verified claims.
Distinguishes structural unconditional gates from replaceable scenario verifiers.
Proposes AI-native durable wiring through a tracked hooks directory plus an AGENTS.md setup line rather than ephemeral .git/hooks.
Mirrors the wire's per-build lint scope in the hook design.
concerns: The hook is presented as untested and needs chmod +x plus an end-to-end git commit test.
The two existing #fff literals are called low-debt without deciding whether they should be fixed now or grandfathered.
The bespoke-elements gate is a stub, so the FORM gate currently covers only colour literals.
notes: A verification finding that confirms the FORM gate fires on wire-routed canvas builds, surfaces the bypass path for direct edits, and supplies a ready-to-install tracked pre-commit hook design.
questions: Has the proposed pre-commit hook been installed and end-to-end tested yet?
Who ensures fresh clones and worktrees run git config core.hooksPath hooks?
Should the two existing #fff literals in app.css be replaced now or treated as grandfathered debt?
What is the graduation plan for the bespoke-elements stub?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — the file answers the stated wiring question and extends into gap analysis and a concrete fix proposal.
apparent_themes: Ground-truth changed-file capture via git diff
AI-native repository hygiene with tracked, self-describing hooks
Fail-safe design that does not gate clean changes on pre-existing debt
intention_signals: It argues that the wire's FORM gate is already structurally correct for approved builds, surfaces the real risk at the commit boundary for edits that bypass the wire, and supplies a minimal tracked pre-commit hook that replicates the wire's per-file lint semantics.
novelty: It treats a pre-commit hook as a tracked, self-documenting AI-native control rather than a local developer convenience, and explicitly copies the wire's 'lint only this build's changed surface files' constraint to avoid legacy debt blocking new commits.
contribution: It contributes a verified, cited answer about FORM gate wiring, names two concrete off-token literals in live canvas source, and provides a ready-to-install hook specification plus setup instructions that extend the wire's lint semantics to every commit path.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a Round 2 verification finding that confirms the design-lint FORM gate fires unconditionally inside the dispatch_decision build path for canvas/ frontend files, identifies that direct edits bypassing dispatch_decision are ungated, reports two existing #fff off-token literals in canvas/app/src/app.css, and proposes a tracked pre-commit hook that lints only staged canvas .tsx/.css files wired via core.hooksPath and AGENTS.md.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to verify FORM gate coverage for the guided-review-surface frontend build path and close the bypass gap with a universal commit-time lint hook.
